bulky.def: 龐大的、笨重的、體積大的的人 (或物)
bulky.ex: I found it difficult to carry such a bulky package alone.

bull.def: 公牛、雄獸、(股市)看好者、吹牛、強悍的人的人物、強悍地 (牛)
bull.col: bull market (牛市), take the bull by the horns (勇敢面對), bull in a china shop
bull.ex: The angry bull chased the hikers across the open field.

bulldog.def: 鬥牛犬、頑強的人、堅韌的人的人物、堅韌地 (堅)
bulldog.ex: The prime minister was famous for his bulldog spirit.

bulldozer.ex: The builder used a heavy bulldozer to clear the site.

bullet.def: 子彈、(物)快車、像子彈般的、子彈地 (彈)
bullet.col: bite the bullet (忍痛應對), silver bullet (良方), bullet point (項次符號)
bullet.ex: The bullet missed the target by only a few inches.

bulletin.def: 公報、公告、簡報、新聞快報、公告的人、公告地 (報)
bulletin.col: news bulletin, parish bulletin, information bulletin
bulletin.ex: We heard a news bulletin about the sudden earthquake.

bulletproof.def: 防彈的、無懈可擊的、防護地 (防彈)
bulletproof.ex: The VIP's car is equipped with bulletproof glass.

bullfight.ex: They went to see a traditional bullfight in Madrid.

bullfighting.def: 鬥牛、鬥牛藝術的人 (或物) (鬥)
bullfighting.ex: Bullfighting is considered a controversial sport by many.

bullfinch.ex: He spotted a beautiful bullfinch in the garden tree.

bullion.def: 金銀條、(衣)金銀絲緣飾 (單數)
bullion.ex: The bank stores massive quantities of pure gold bullion.

bullish.def: 像牛的、(股市)看漲的、樂觀的
bullish.ex: Investors are feeling quite bullish about the technology sector.

bullock.ex: A team of working bullocks pulled the heavy cart.

bullring.ex: The historic bullring was packed with excited spectators.

bull's-eye/bullseye.def: 靶心、正中目標、靶心的人物 (靶)
bull's-eye/bullseye.ex: The archer hit the bull's-eye with his very first arrow.

bully.def: 霸凌者、惡霸、霸凌、恐嚇、特好的、霸凌的人地 (霸)
bully.col: school bully, cyber bully, bully for you! (好樣的)
bully.ex: Don't let that workplace bully get the better of you.

bullying.def: 霸凌行為、恐嚇、霸凌的人 (或物) (霸)
bullying.ex: Schools should have a zero-tolerance policy towards bullying.

bulrush.ex: The baby birds were hiding in the clump of bulrushes.

bulwark.def: 堡壘、防禦物、橫木、守護的人 (或物)、防禦地 (堡)
bulwark.ex: The stone wall served as a protective bulwark for the town.

bum.def: (俚)屁股、流浪漢、乞求、拙劣的、浪費地 (屁)
bum.col: beach bum, bum around (遊手好閒), bum a ride (搭便車)
bum.ex: He spent the whole summer just bumming around the beach.

bumble.def: 含糊不清地說、拙劣地做、大黃蜂、拙劣的人、含糊地 (糊)
bumble.ex: He managed to bumble through his very first public speech.

bumblebee.ex: A fuzzy bumblebee was buzzing around the garden flowers.

bumbling.def: 笨手笨腳的、裝模作樣的的人 (或物) (笨)
bumbling.ex: The bumbling detective eventually solved the case by luck.

bummed.ex: I was really bummed out that I couldn't go to the party.

bump.def: 重擊、腫塊、路面隆起、碰撞、顛簸、碰撞的人、重擊地 (碰)
bump.col: speed bump, goose bumps (雞皮疙瘩), bump into (巧遇)
bump.ex: The car hit a bump in the gravel road.

bumper.def: (車)保險桿、特大的、豐盛的、豐盛的人 (或物) (保驗桿)
bumper.col: bumper harvest (大豐收), bumper to bumper (塞車)
bumper.ex: The farmers are celebrating a record bumper harvest this year.

bumping.ex: The bumping sounds from downstairs kept her awake all night.

bumpkin.ex: He felt like a country bumpkin in the big city.

bumptious.def: 傲慢的、自大的、自誇地 (傲)
bumptious.ex: No one liked him because of his loud and bumptious manner.

bumpy.def: 崎嶇不平的、顛簸的的人 (或物)
bumpy.ex: The flight was a bit bumpy due to the mountain weather.

bun.def: 小圓麵包、(髮)髮髻、(俚)屁股、髮髻的人、小圓地 (圓)
bun.col: hamburger bun, hair in a bun, cinnamon bun
bun.ex: She tied her long hair back in a neat bun.

bunch.def: 串、束、群、成群、在群中人物、成束地 (束)
bunch.col: a bunch of flowers, a bunch of keys, thanks a bunch! (謝啦)
bunch.ex: He bought a large bunch of bananas at the market.

bundle.def: 捆、束、一大筆錢、紮、大筆的人、成捆地 (捆)
bundle.col: bundle of joy (小孩), bundle up (裹嚴實), a bundle of nerves
bundle.ex: She carried a large bundle of firewood to the house.

bung.def: 塞子、(口)賄賂、塞住、(口)投擲、塞子的人、塞住地 (塞)
bung.col: bung hole, bung up (弄糟/堵死)
bung.ex: He used a wooden bung to seal the wine barrel.

bungalow.ex: The elderly couple decided to move into a cozy bungalow.

bunging.ex: She spent the morning bunging old clothes into boxes.

bungle.def: 搞砸、笨手笨腳地做、拙劣的工作、搞砸的人、笨拙地 (砸)
bungle.ex: The burglars managed to bungle the entire library robbery.

bungler.def: 笨手笨腳的人、搞砸的人 (單數)
bungler.ex: The incompetent bungler made ten mistakes in one hour.

bungling.def: 拙劣的、笨拙的的人 (或物) (拙)
bungling.ex: The project failed because of the bungling management team.

bunion.def: (腳)拇指黏液囊腫、腳痛的人 (單數)
bunion.ex: She had surgery to correct a very painful bunion.

bunk.def: 臥舖、(口)胡說、(俚)逃跑、鋪在臥舖裡的人物、臥舖地 (臥舖)
bunk.col: bunk beds, do a bunk (潛逃), that's total bunk!
bunk.ex: The children shared a room with comfortable bunk beds.

bunker.def: 地堡、掩體、(高爾夫)沙坑、儲藏室的人物、地堡地 (地堡)
bunker.col: underground bunker, sand bunker, coal bunker
bunker.ex: The soldiers took cover in an old underground bunker.
